resources.description: One catalog entity as markdown: identity, owner, relations, provenance, doc index, and the overview doc body (byte-capped). `entityKey` is base64url(entityRef). The agent-facing twin of the entity page; use `catalog_search` to find refs.
resources.name: catalog_entity
resources.title: Catalog entity overview

resources.description: One delivery run as markdown: status, git provenance, timings, and the plan-DAG job list with per-job statuses. Use `runs_list` to find run ids, and the `runs_read_logs` tool for a job's log output.
resources.name: run_summary
resources.title: Run summary

tools.description: Identify the authenticated actor and list the workspaces they belong to (with roles where the API reports them). Call this first to orient before any workspace-scoped tool; it takes no arguments.
tools.name: whoami
tools.title: Who am I

tools.description: List the workspaces the caller is a member of: slugs, `ws_…` refs, and kind (account/workspace). Use the returned slug or ref as the `workspace` argument of every scoped tool; not needed if `whoami` was already called.
tools.name: workspaces_list
tools.title: List workspaces

tools.description: List the projects (git repos) in a workspace. Pass `project` (id or slug) to narrow to one project and inline its environments; without the filter environments are omitted to keep the call cheap.

project.description: Optional project filter: a `prj_…` id or project slug. When set, the matched project's environments are included.

workspace.description: Workspace to read from: a `ws_…` workspace id, a workspace slug, or a legacy `org_…` id (passed through verbatim; the API resolves it). Use `whoami` or `workspaces_list` to discover yours.

tools.name: projects_list
tools.title: List projects

tools.description: Search the workspace's org-wide service catalog (the merged, git-derived component graph). Facets: `kind`, `owner`, `project`, `environment`; `q` free-text matches name or entityRef. For one known entityRef use `catalog_get_entity` instead.

cursor.description: Opaque continuation cursor from a previous page's `meta.cursor`; pass back verbatim.

limit.description: Page size (the server clamps to its own bounds).

project.description: Project (repo) public id (`prj_…`). Use `projects_list` to discover ids.

tools.description: Fetch one catalog entity by its exact entityRef (e.g. `component:default/api`): identity, owner, relations, and provenance. Use `catalog_search` when you don't know the exact ref.

project.description: Optional provenance filter (`prj_…`) when the ref exists in several projects.

workspace.description: Workspace to read from: a `ws_…` workspace id, a workspace slug, or a legacy `org_…` id (passed through verbatim; the API resolves it). Use `whoami` or `workspaces_list` to discover yours.

tools.description: Browse the workspace's git-authored catalog docs (filter by `entityRef`, `role`, `project`, `q`), or pass `digest` (from a browse row or an entity's docRef) to read one doc's markdown body, byte-capped. Not for run logs — use `runs_read_logs`.

cursor.description: Opaque continuation cursor from a previous page's `meta.cursor`; pass back verbatim.

digest.description: Content digest (`sha256:…`) of a doc body to read. When set, all browse filters are ignored.

entityRef.description: Narrow the doc index to one entity's doc set.

limit.description: Page size (the server clamps to its own bounds).

project.description: Project (repo) public id (`prj_…`). Use `projects_list` to discover ids.

role.description: Doc role slug (overview | guide | runbook | architecture | …).

workspace.description: Workspace to read from: a `ws_…` workspace id, a workspace slug, or a legacy `org_…` id (passed through verbatim; the API resolves it). Use `whoami` or `workspaces_list` to discover yours.

cursor.description: Opaque continuation cursor from a previous page's `meta.cursor`; pass back verbatim.

limit.description: Page size (the server clamps to its own bounds).

fromSeq.description: Resume cursor: the `nextSeq` returned by a previous call. Defaults to 0 (start).

limit.description: Page size (the server clamps to its own bounds).

cursor.description: Opaque continuation cursor from a previous page's `meta.cursor`; pass back verbatim.

eventId.description: Fetch exactly this event by id (`evt_…`); other filters are ignored.

limit.description: Page size (the server clamps to its own bounds).

cursor.description: Opaque continuation cursor from a previous page's `meta.cursor`; pass back verbatim.

limit.description: Page size (the server clamps to its own bounds).

tools.description: Answer "who can do what here, and via which grant": effective permissions with provenance (direct / team / account-cascade) plus the workspace's member and team rosters. project.description: Project (repo) public id (`prj_…`). Use `projects_list` to discover ids.

subjectId.description: Subject to explain (`usr_…` / `sp_…`). Defaults to the caller.

tools.description: Read the settings and feature flags at one config scope — organization (default), project, or project+environment. Never returns secret values; for secret metadata use `secrets_list`.

environment.description: Environment scope (public id or slug); requires `project`.

project.description: Project scope (`prj_…`). Omit for organization scope.

tools.description: List secret METADATA (keys, versions, rotation state) at one config scope. Secret values are write-only platform-wide: no tool, flag, or argument can return one. Use `config_read` for settings/flags.

environment.description: Environment scope (public id or slug); requires `project`.

project.description: Project scope (`prj_…`). Omit for organization scope.

cursor.description: Opaque continuation cursor from a previous page's `meta.cursor`; pass back verbatim.

endpoint.description: Webhook endpoint id. Omit to list endpoints instead of deliveries.

limit.description: Page size (the server clamps to its own bounds).

tools.title: List webhook deliveries

tools.description: Create a new project (git repo) in a workspace. This is a WRITE: it is policy-gated (requires a builder-or-higher role) and audited like any console/CLI mutation. Retries are replay-safe — an Idempotency-Key is generated per call unless you supply `idempotencyKey`. To browse existing projects use `projects_list` instead.

idempotencyKey.description: Optional Idempotency-Key for this write (1-255 printable ASCII chars). Omit it and the tool generates a fresh key per call; supply the SAME key when retrying a call whose outcome you did not observe, so the platform replays the original result instead of double-creating.

slug.description: URL-safe slug. Omit to let the API derive one from the name.

workspace.description: Workspace to read from: a `ws_…` workspace id, a workspace slug, or a legacy `org_…` id (passed through verbatim; the API resolves it). Use `whoami` or `workspaces_list` to discover yours.

tools.name: project_create
tools.title: Create project

tools.description: Create a new environment (e.g. staging) under a project. This is a WRITE: policy-gated (builder-or-higher role) and audited like any console/CLI mutation. Retries are replay-safe — an Idempotency-Key is generated per call unless you supply `idempotencyKey`. To browse existing environments use `projects_list` with the `project` filter.

idempotencyKey.description: Optional Idempotency-Key for this write (1-255 printable ASCII chars). Omit it and the tool generates a fresh key per call; supply the SAME key when retrying a call whose outcome you did not observe, so the platform replays the original result instead of double-creating.

project.description: Project (repo) public id (`prj_…`). Use `projects_list` to discover ids.

slug.description: URL-safe slug. Omit to let the API derive one from the name.

workspace.description: Workspace to read from: a `ws_…` workspace id, a workspace slug, or a legacy `org_…` id (passed through verbatim; the API resolves it). Use `whoami` or `workspaces_list` to discover yours.

tools.name: environment_create
tools.title: Create environment

tools.description: Set a feature flag at one config scope — organization (default), project, or project+environment: updates `enabled` and/or `value` for `flagKey`, creating the flag at that scope when it does not exist yet. This is a WRITE: policy-gated (builder-or-higher role) and audited like any console/CLI mutation; retries are replay-safe (an Idempotency-Key is generated per call unless you supply `idempotencyKey`). To read flags use `config_read`.

enabled.description: Turn the flag on or off. At least one of `enabled`/`value` is required.

environment.description: Environment scope (public id or slug); requires `project`.

idempotencyKey.description: Optional Idempotency-Key for this write (1-255 printable ASCII chars). Omit it and the tool generates a fresh key per call; supply the SAME key when retrying a call whose outcome you did not observe, so the platform replays the original result instead of double-creating.

project.description: Project scope (`prj_…`). Omit for organization scope.

value.description: Optional JSON payload served with the flag (any JSON value).

workspace.description: Workspace to read from: a `ws_…` workspace id, a workspace slug, or a legacy `org_…` id (passed through verbatim; the API resolves it). Use `whoami` or `workspaces_list` to discover yours.

tools.name: flag_set
tools.title: Set feature flag

tools.description: Create a webhook endpoint (delivery URL) in a workspace — optionally project-scoped via `project` — and subscribe it to the given `events` types in the same call. This is a WRITE: policy-gated (builder-or-higher role) and audited like any console/CLI mutation; retries are replay-safe (an Idempotency-Key is generated per call unless you supply `idempotencyKey`). Signing-secret material is never returned. To inspect deliveries use `webhook_deliveries_list`.

events.description: Event types to subscribe the endpoint to (e.g. `run.completed`); each becomes a webhook subscription. Omit to create the endpoint with no subscriptions (it will receive nothing until subscribed).

idempotencyKey.description: Optional Idempotency-Key for this write (1-255 printable ASCII chars). Omit it and the tool generates a fresh key per call; supply the SAME key when retrying a call whose outcome you did not observe, so the platform replays the original result instead of double-creating.

project.description: Optional project scope (`prj_…`). Omit for a workspace-level endpoint.

url.description: Delivery URL the platform will POST signed events to.

workspace.description: Workspace to read from: a `ws_…` workspace id, a workspace slug, or a legacy `org_…` id (passed through verbatim; the API resolves it). Use `whoami` or `workspaces_list` to discover yours.

tools.name: webhook_create
tools.title: Create webhook endpoint

tools.description: Re-send a past webhook delivery attempt to its endpoint through the normal signing/delivery path; returns the NEW attempt with its post-delivery status (the original is unchanged). Find attempt ids (`wha_…`) with `webhook_deliveries_list`. This is a WRITE: policy-gated and audited; retries are replay-safe (an Idempotency-Key is generated per call unless you supply `idempotencyKey`).

idempotencyKey.description: Optional Idempotency-Key for this write (1-255 printable ASCII chars). Omit it and the tool generates a fresh key per call; supply the SAME key when retrying a call whose outcome you did not observe, so the platform replays the original result instead of double-creating.

workspace.description: Workspace to read from: a `ws_…` workspace id, a workspace slug, or a legacy `org_…` id (passed through verbatim; the API resolves it). Use `whoami` or `workspaces_list` to discover yours.

tools.name: webhook_delivery_replay
tools.title: Replay webhook delivery

tools.description: Invite a person to a workspace by email with an organization role (they join once they accept). This is a WRITE: policy-gated (admin-or-higher role) and audited like any console/CLI mutation; retries are replay-safe (an Idempotency-Key is generated per call unless you supply `idempotencyKey`). The one-time accept token is NOT returned — the invitee accepts via their own signed-in session. To review members and grants use `access_explain`.

idempotencyKey.description: Optional Idempotency-Key for this write (1-255 printable ASCII chars). Omit it and the tool generates a fresh key per call; supply the SAME key when retrying a call whose outcome you did not observe, so the platform replays the original result instead of double-creating.

role.description: Organization role granted on acceptance. Prefer the least-privileged role that fits (`viewer` for read-only access).

workspace.description: Workspace to read from: a `ws_…` workspace id, a workspace slug, or a legacy `org_…` id (passed through verbatim; the API resolves it). Use `whoami` or `workspaces_list` to discover yours.
